agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive courier service with a sub-2-hour delivery window, premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a refund policy for missed delivery windows. Cross-functional stakeholders have provided raw requirements with significant contradictions that must be resolved before implementation.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Service — a sub-domain within Order Fulfillment that governs eligibility, dispatch, SLA enforcement, and refund processing for premium time-guaranteed deliveries.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option promising a sub-2-hour delivery window from order placement to delivery completion, available to eligible customers for an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee when a customer selects Express Delivery." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery speed tier." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour time period starting from order placement within which the express delivery must be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to physical pickup of the package by the assigned driver." },
      { "term": "Warehouse Proximity", "definition": "The 10km maximum straight-line or road distance between the customer's delivery address and the nearest active warehouse, used to determine Express Delivery eligibility." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Missed Window", "definition": "The event that occurs when an express delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering the refund evaluation process." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation", "definition": "The event when an assigned driver abandons an in-progress express delivery, triggering the 10-minute replacement assignment window." },
      { "term": "Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute period after a driver cancellation within which a new express-eligible driver must be assigned." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "DISPUTED TERM — The express fee refund issued when a delivery window is missed. Legal frames this as discretionary goodwill; PM and Customer Success frame it as an automatic guarantee. Must be resolved." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Exclusion", "definition": "A refund exclusion condition where the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, voiding the delivery window commitment." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Express Delivery is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — a 2-hour window is operationally impossible beyond this radius given pickup, transit, and delivery time. Violating this corrupts the feasibility of every downstream SLA.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system MUST reject Express Delivery selection when the delivery address is more than 10km from the nearest warehouse."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to Express Delivery orders.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational quality constraint — assigning a lower-rated driver to a time-critical delivery violates the service quality contract and increases missed-window risk. A sub-4.0 driver on an express order is a system integrity violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "The system MUST NOT assign an express order to a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up by the assigned driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint directly tied to the 2-hour delivery window feasibility. If pickup exceeds 15 minutes, the remaining transit and delivery time budget is compromised, making the window unachievable.",
      "testable_assertion": "If an express order has not been picked up within 15 minutes of placement, the system MUST escalate (reassign or alert operations)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that can be changed by product/finance leadership. The amount is not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a customer selects Express Delivery, the order total MUST include the configured express fee on top of the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When a delivery window is missed, only the express fee is refunded — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund scope is a business policy decision. Finance or leadership could change the refund to include the base fee in the future.",
      "testable_assertion": "A missed-window refund MUST equal exactly the express fee amount and MUST NOT include the base delivery fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the delivery window is missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a refund policy exclusion, changeable by business decision.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a missed window is attributed to an incorrect customer address, the system MUST NOT issue a refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 10-minute window is an operational target, not a physical or data-integrity constraint. It could be adjusted based on operational capacity.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancels an in-progress express delivery, the system MUST attempt replacement assignment and track elapsed time against the 10-minute threshold."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Real-time driver tracking must be available to the customer for all express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "A product feature requirement, not a data integrity invariant. The delivery can still be completed without it.",
      "testable_assertion": "While an express delivery is in progress, the customer MUST be able to view the assigned driver's location updated at intervals no greater than [TBD — see gaps]."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Recommend inclusive (<=10km) to avoid customer frustration at boundary." },
    { "scenario": "Driver rating drops below 4.0 during an active express delivery", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Should the delivery continue with the current driver or trigger reassignment? Recommend: complete current delivery, exclude driver from future express assignments." },
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers available within 15-minute pickup window", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Should the order be downgraded to standard with express fee refunded, or held in queue? This is a gap — no stakeholder addressed this." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels and no replacement is available within 10 minutes", "expected_behavior": "Customer Success says auto-refund. But: is the delivery also cancelled, or does a standard driver eventually complete it? Refund scope unclear (express fee only? full amount?)." },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to a location >10km from warehouse", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Should the express option be revoked? Should the delivery window reset? No stakeholder addressed mid-delivery address changes." },
    { "scenario": "Missed window caused by force majeure (weather, road closure, etc.)", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Legal's 'best effort' framing suggests no obligation, but PM/CS imply automatic refund. No exclusion policy defined for external factors." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple driver cancellations on the same express order", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: Does the 10-minute replacement window reset per cancellation? Is there a maximum number of reassignment attempts before auto-cancellation?" },
    { "scenario": "Order placed when 2-hour window would cross midnight or business hours boundary", "expected_behavior": "NEEDS CLARIFICATION: No stakeholder mentioned operating hours for express. Is express available 24/7? If not, what happens when the window would expire outside operating hours?" },
    { "scenario": "Refund processing for missed window where customer also gave wrong address", "expected_behavior": "Wrong Address Exclusion (BR-6) takes precedence — no refund. But: who determines 'wrong address'? What is the dispute mechanism? Needs process definition." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) represents incremental revenue per order but carries refund liability for missed windows. The refund policy's legal framing (goodwill vs guarantee) has material P&L implications — if treated as a guarantee, refund rates could be significant. The wrong-address exclusion is a refund cost control mechanism. Refund processing within 3 business days creates a cash flow timing consideration.",
    "operational": "15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the eligible driver pool. Operations must maintain sufficient express-eligible driver density within 10km of each warehouse. Driver cancellation replacement within 10 minutes requires sophisticated real-time dispatch. This product will create operational pressure to maintain driver ratings and geographic coverage.",
    "user": "Real-time tracking and prominent 2-hour display set high customer expectations. The gap between marketing language ('guaranteed') and legal reality ('best effort') creates trust risk. Automatic refund on driver cancellation is a strong customer-friendly signal, but the wrong-address exclusion could create disputes and negative sentiment if not communicated clearly at checkout.",
    "risk": "HIGH — The fundamental contradiction between 'guarantee' and 'best effort' creates legal exposure if marketed as guaranteed but governed as discretionary. Inconsistent refund triggers (missed window vs driver cancellation) could lead to customer confusion and support overhead. The 10km radius constraint limits addressable market.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "New premium option with time commitment and real-time visibility, but confusing refund terms if guarantee/best-effort contradiction is not resolved. Wrong-address exclusion needs transparent communication." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "4-star minimum creates a two-tier driver system. Express-eligible drivers face higher performance pressure (15-min pickup). Cancellation penalty implications are undefined." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "Must build real-time SLA monitoring, automated escalation at 15-min pickup breach, and 10-minute replacement dispatch. Significant new operational complexity." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "New revenue line (express fee) offset by refund liability. Must build refund processing within 3-business-day SLA. Need forecasting for refund rates." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, missed-window refund timing, and the guarantee-vs-best-effort confusion. Need clear playbooks." },
      { "group": "Legal / Compliance", "impact": "Must review all customer-facing language to ensure no contractual guarantee is implied. Refund terms must be framed as policy, not obligation." },
      { "group": "Marketing", "impact": "Cannot use 'guaranteed' in advertising if Legal's position holds. Must align messaging with the resolved framing." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is very low due to a fundamental unresolved contradiction between PM ('guaranteed 2 hours') and Legal ('best effort, not a guarantee'). This is not a minor wording issue — it changes the entire product positioning, refund obligation model, and customer-facing language. Additionally, Customer Success's automatic refund on driver cancellation conflicts with Finance's 3-business-day processing timeline and the unclear refund trigger model. Until these contradictions are resolved at the leadership level, no implementation can be considered correct.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "10km warehouse proximity as an eligibility constraint",
      "Express fee as an additive charge on top of base delivery fee",
      "4-star minimum driver rating for express eligibility",
      "15-minute pickup SLA as an operational necessity",
      "Wrong-address exclusion from refund eligibility"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this changes everything",
      "Whether refunds are automatic or discretionary",
      "Refund processing: immediate/automatic vs 3-business-day processing",
      "What happens when no replacement driver is available within 10 minutes",
      "Operating hours and availability windows for express service",
      "Real-time tracking update frequency requirements",
      "Dispute resolution process for wrong-address determinations"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km distance is measured as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not road distance — needs confirmation",
      "Driver rating is calculated as a rolling average, not a per-delivery score — needs confirmation",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time — inferred from Operations Lead's comment but not explicitly stated",
      "Express delivery operates within the same hours as standard delivery — no stakeholder mentioned dedicated hours",
      "A single warehouse serves as the origin — multi-warehouse routing for express is not discussed"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "CRITICAL: Product Manager states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed' and the refund is automatic on missed windows. Legal explicitly states this is 'best effort' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are mutually exclusive positions. If we build a guarantee, Legal is exposed. If we build best-effort, PM's product promise is broken. This must be resolved at the executive level before any implementation begins.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Legal", "Product Management", "Marketing", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Guarantee model — 2-hour commitment with automatic refund, accepted legal risk, requires strong exclusion clauses",
        "B: Best-effort model — 2-hour target with discretionary refund, lower legal risk, weaker customer value proposition",
        "C: Hybrid — 'Service Level Target' with automatic refund triggered by system (not contractual), with exclusions for wrong address, force majeure, etc."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — frame as a 'Service Level Target' with automatic but policy-based (not contractually guaranteed) refunds. This gives customers the experience of a guarantee while preserving Legal's position. All customer-facing copy must avoid the word 'guarantee.' Requires Legal sign-off on specific language."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refunds should be 'automatic' when a replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes. Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These are incompatible — either the refund is instant/automatic or it goes through a processing queue. This also raises the question: is the driver-cancellation refund a different refund type than the missed-window refund?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Finance", "Customer Support", "Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: All refunds are automatic and instant (credit to account/wallet immediately)",
        "B: All refunds go through 3-business-day processing regardless of trigger",
        "C: Refunds are auto-approved immediately but payment processing takes up to 3 business days (customer sees 'refund approved' instantly but funds arrive later)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — separate refund approval (immediate, system-triggered) from refund settlement (up to 3 business days for payment processing). This satisfies Customer Success's 'automatic' requirement and Finance's processing timeline. Customer communication should say 'Refund approved — funds will appear within 3 business days.'"
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "No stakeholder defined what happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached and no driver has picked up the order. Is the order auto-cancelled? Downgraded to standard? Held in queue with an extended window? This is a critical operational gap — it's the most likely failure mode for express delivery.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-cancel express, refund express fee, offer to convert to standard delivery",
        "B: Extend window and keep trying to assign an express driver",
        "C: Notify customer of delay and let them choose (cancel express or wait)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — auto-downgrade to standard with immediate express fee refund and proactive customer notification. This preserves trust and avoids compounding a missed pickup into a missed delivery window."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed express delivery operating hours, geographic rollout scope, order volume caps, or surge pricing. If express is available 24/7, the driver pool constraint (4-star, within 10km) becomes much harder to satisfy during off-peak hours. Volume caps may be needed to prevent over-commitment during peak periods.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations", "Customers", "Finance", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Limit express to defined operating hours (e.g., 8am-10pm)",
        "B: Available 24/7 but with dynamic eligibility based on driver availability",
        "C: Available 24/7 with surge pricing during off-peak to offset operational cost"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Gather data from Operations on driver availability patterns before deciding. At minimum, the system should dynamically disable express when no eligible drivers are available within the service area, rather than accepting orders it cannot fulfill."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Who determines that a customer gave a 'wrong address' and how? This is the sole refund exclusion mentioned, but there is no process for adjudicating it. Without a clear determination process, this exclusion will become a major source of customer disputes and support escalations.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Customer Support", "Legal"],
      "options": [
        "A: Driver reports wrong address via app, triggers support review",
        "B: System validates address at checkout against geocoding service, preventing wrong addresses upfront",
        "C: Both — validate at checkout AND allow driver to flag, with support arbitration for disputes"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C — address validation at checkout reduces wrong-address incidents, driver flagging catches edge cases, and support arbitration provides a dispute path. The system should log GPS evidence (driver location vs delivery address) to support adjudication."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot proceed to implementation with 'guarantee' language until Legal and PM align on SLA framing", "business_rule_broken": "Fundamental product positioning is contradicted between stakeholders — building either interpretation without resolution will produce a system that one stakeholder considers incorrect" },
    { "violation": "Refund trigger model is undefined — automatic vs processed, and which events trigger refunds", "business_rule_broken": "BR-5 and BR-7 cannot both be implemented without knowing whether refunds are instant or queued, and whether driver-cancellation refunds follow the same rules as missed-window refunds" }
  ]
}
